    97→#'                  Must be NULL, a single contrast spec, or a *named* list of contrast specs.
    98→#' @param id a unique \code{character} identifier used to refer to term, otherwise will be determined from variable names.
    99→#' @param name Optional human-readable name for the term.
   100→#' @param lag a temporal offset in seconds which is added to onset before convolution
   101→#' @param summate whether impulse amplitudes sum up when duration is greater than 0.
   102→#' @examples 
   103→#' 
   104→#' ## 'hrf' is typically used in the context of \code{formula}s passed to `event_model`.
   105→#' 
   106→#' # Simple model with one factor
   107→#' form1 <- onsets ~ hrf(condition, basis="spmg1")
   108→#' 
   109→#' # Model with factor and continuous modulator, using default SPMG1 for both terms
   110→#' form2 <- onsets ~ hrf(condition) + hrf(RT)
   111→#' 
   112→#' # Model with interaction term and SPMG3 basis
   113→#' form3 <- onsets ~ hrf(condition, RT, basis="spmg3")
   114→#' 
   115→#' # Model with an expression and contrasts
   116→#' library(rlang)
   117→#' con1 <- pair_contrast(~ condition == "A", ~ condition == "B", name="AvB")
   118→#' form4 <- onsets ~ hrf(condition, Poly(RT, 2), contrasts=con1)
   119→#' 
   120→#' @export
   121→#' @importFrom rlang enquos enexpr syms is_formula is_quosure is_call as_label %||%
   122→#' @return an \code{hrfspec} instance
   123→hrf <- function(..., basis="spmg1", onsets=NULL, durations=NULL, prefix=NULL, subset=NULL, precision=.3, 
   124→                nbasis=1, contrasts=NULL, id=NULL, name=NULL, lag=0, summate=TRUE) {
   125→  
   126→  vars <- rlang::enquos(...) # Capture variables/expressions as quosures
   127→  
   128→  # --- Handle special named arguments within ... ---
   129→  var_names <- names(vars)
   130→  # Remove any quosures whose name matches hrf formals (these are control args, not vars)
   131→  hrf_formals <- names(formals(hrf))
   132→  var_indices <- ! (var_names %in% hrf_formals)
   133→  vars <- vars[var_indices]
   134→  
   135→  if (length(vars) == 0) {
   136→      stop("`hrf` must have at least one variable or expression specified in `...`")
   137→  }
   138→  
   139→  # --- Determine Term ID/Name (Prioritize id, then name, then auto) --- 
   140→  final_id <- id # Prioritize explicit id
   141→  if (is.null(final_id) && !is.null(name)) { # Use name if id is missing
   142→      final_id <- name
   143→  }
   144→  # final_id remains NULL if neither id nor name provided, hrfspec will generate default
   145→  
   146→  # --- Check contrasts argument --- 
   147→  if (!is.null(contrasts)) {
   148→    if (!is.list(contrasts)) {
   149→        if (inherits(contrasts, "contrast_spec")) {
   150→            contrast_list <- list(contrasts)
   151→            cname <- contrasts$name %||% "contrast1"
   152→            names(contrast_list) <- cname
   153→            contrasts <- contrast_list
   154→        } else {
   155→             stop("`contrasts=` argument must be a single contrast_spec object or a list of them.\n",
   156→                  "  Detected type: ", class(contrasts)[1], "\n",
   157→                  "  Hint: Use functions like contrast(), pair_contrast(), contrast_set() to define contrasts.", 
   158→                  call. = FALSE)
   159→        }
   160→    } else {
   161→        is_spec <- vapply(contrasts, inherits, logical(1), "contrast_spec")
   162→        if (!all(is_spec)) {
   163→             stop("If `contrasts=` is a list, all elements must be contrast_spec objects.\n",
   164→                  "  Hint: Use functions like contrast(), pair_contrast(), contrast_set() to define contrasts.", 
   165→                  call. = FALSE)
   166→        }
   167→        if (is.null(names(contrasts)) || any(names(contrasts) == "")) {
   168→            cnames <- sapply(contrasts, function(cs) cs$name %||% paste0("contrast", which(sapply(contrasts, identical, cs))))
   169→             if (any(duplicated(cnames))) {
   170→                stop("If `contrasts=` is a list, it must be a *named* list (or auto-naming must yield unique names).", call. = FALSE)
   171→            }
   172→            names(contrasts) <- cnames
   173→        }
   174→    }
   175→  }
   176→  # -----------------------------
   177→  
   178→  basis_obj <- make_hrf(basis, lag, nbasis=nbasis)
   179→  
   180→  # Call the internal constructor, passing quosures directly
   181→  ret <- hrfspec(
   182→    vars = vars, # Pass list of quosures captured by enquos
   183→    basis = basis_obj,         
   184→    onsets = onsets,       
   185→    durations = durations, 
   186→    prefix = prefix,       
   187→    subset = rlang::enexpr(subset), # Capture subset expr unevaluated
   188→    precision = precision,
   189→    contrasts = contrasts, ## Pass validated list of contrast specs
   190→    summate = summate,
   191→    id = final_id # Pass the determined ID 
   192→    )
   193→
   194→  ret
   195→}
   196→
   197→
   198→#' Internal constructor for hrfspec objects
   199→#' 
   200→#' Creates the hrfspec list structure. Called by `hrf()`.
   201→#' Generates termname and label from the input variables/expressions.
   202→#'
   203→#' @param vars List of quosures representing variables/expressions.
   204→#' @param label Optional label for the term (if NULL, generated automatically).
   205→#' @param basis An `HRF` object.
   206→#' @param ... Other arguments passed from `hrf()` (onsets, durations, prefix, subset, etc.)
   207→#' @return An `hrfspec` instance (list with class `hrfspec`).
   208→#' @importFrom rlang as_label is_symbol is_call quo_get_expr
   209→#' @noRd
   210→#' @keywords internal
   211→hrfspec <- function(vars, label=NULL, basis=fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1, ...) {
   212→
   213→  assert_that(inherits(basis, "HRF"))
   214→  
   215→  # Generate varnames and termname from quosures
   216→  var_labels <- sapply(vars, rlang::as_label)
   217→  # Simple heuristic for names: use symbol directly, otherwise make.names on label
   218→  varnames <- sapply(vars, function(q) {
   219→       expr <- rlang::quo_get_expr(q)
   220→       if (rlang::is_symbol(expr)) as.character(expr) else make.names(rlang::as_label(expr))
   221→  })
   222→  
   223→  termname <- paste0(varnames, collapse=":")
   224→  
   225→  # Generate label if not provided
   226→  if (is.null(label)) {
   227→      label <- paste0("hrf(", paste0(var_labels, collapse=","), ")")
   228→  }
   229→  
   230→  # Capture other arguments passed via ... from hrf()
   231→  other_args <- list(...)
   232→  
   233→  ret <- list(
   234→    name = termname, 
   235→    label = label,
   236→    id = other_args$id,
   237→    vars = vars,
   238→    varnames = varnames, 
   239→    hrf = basis,
   240→    onsets = other_args$onsets,
   241→    durations = other_args$durations,
   242→    prefix = other_args$prefix,
   243→    subset = other_args$subset, # Should be an expression
   244→    precision = other_args$precision %||% 0.3,
   245→    contrasts = other_args$contrasts, # Already validated list or NULL
   246→    summate = other_args$summate %||% TRUE
   247→    # data_env is NOT stored here, added later during evaluation
   248→  )
   249→  
   250→  # If user supplied an explicit id (or via name= alias), use it as the primary term name
   251→  if (!is.null(other_args$id)) {
   252→      ret$name <- other_args$id
   253→  }
   254→  
   255→  class(ret) <- c("hrfspec", "list")
   256→  ret
   257→}
   258→
   259→
   260→
   261→#' Get number of basis functions from hrfspec
   262→#'
   263→#' @param x An hrfspec object
   264→#' @param ... Additional arguments (unused)
   265→#' @return The number of basis functions
   266→#' @export
   267→nbasis.hrfspec <- function(x, ...) {
   268→  fmrihrf::nbasis(x$hrf)
   269→}
   270→
   271→#' Retrieve contrast specifications from an hrfspec
   272→#'
   273→#' @param x An `hrfspec` object.
   274→#' @param ... Unused.
   275→#' @return The list of contrast specifications attached to the hrfspec, or `NULL`.
   276→#' @export
   277→contrasts.hrfspec <- function(x, ...) {
   278→  x$contrasts
   279→}
   280→
   281→#' @export
   282→construct.hrfspec <- function(x, model_spec, ...) {
   283→  ons <- if (!is.null(x$onsets)) x$onsets else model_spec$onsets
   284→  et <- construct_event_term(x, model_spec)
   285→  
   286→  # Set the term_tag attribute on the event_term before returning
   287→  # This ensures that when convolution happens later, the correct column names are generated
   288→  term_tag <- x$id %||% x$name
   289→  attr(et, "term_tag") <- term_tag
   290→  
   291→  # DON'T convolve here - let build_event_model_design_matrix handle convolution
   292→  # Just return the event_term with hrfspec attached
   293→  # The hrfspec is already attached in construct_event_term
   294→  
   295→  # Handle add_sum flag if present in the hrfspec (set by trialwise)
   296→  # Store this as an attribute for later processing during convolution
   297→  if (isTRUE(x$add_sum)) {
   298→    attr(et, "add_sum") <- TRUE
   299→    attr(et, "add_sum_label") <- x$id %||% x$name
   300→  }
   301→
   302→  # Return the event_term directly, not a convolved_term
   303→  et
   304→}
   305→
   306→#' evaluate.hrfspec
   307→#'
   308→#' This function evaluates a hemodynamic response function (HRF) specified by an hrfspec object for a given set of time points (grid) and other parameters.
   309→#' It is a wrapper function that calls the evaluate.HRF function with the HRF function contained in the hrfspec object.
   310→#'
   311→#' @param x The hrfspec object containing the HRF function.
   312→#' @param grid A vector of time points.
   313→#' @param amplitude The scaling value for the event (default: 1).
   314→#' @param duration The duration of the event (default: 0).
   315→#' @param precision The temporal resolution used for computing summed responses when duration > 0 (default: 0.1).
   316→#' @param ... Additional arguments to be passed to the evaluate.HRF function.
   317→#' @return A vector of HRF values at the specified time points.
   318→#' @noRd
   319→evaluate.hrfspec <- function(x, grid, amplitude=1, duration=0, precision=.1, ...) {
   320→  fmrihrf::evaluate(x$hrf, grid, amplitude, duration, precision)
   321→}
   322→
   323→#' trialwise
   324→#'
   325→#' Generate one regressor per trial (plus an optional grand-mean column)
   326→#' by delegating everything to `hrf()`.
   327→#'
   328→#' Use it **only on the RHS** of an event-model formula:
   329→#'
   330→#'     onset ~ trialwise(basis = "spmg1", add_sum = TRUE)
   331→#'
   332→#' @param basis,lag,nbasis Passed straight to `hrf()`.
   333→#' @param add_sum If TRUE, append a column that is the average of all
   334→#'                trialwise columns (useful as a conventional main effect).
   335→#' @param label Term label / prefix for the generated columns.
   336→#' @export
   337→trialwise <- function(basis   = "spmg1",
   338→                      lag     = 0,
   339→                      nbasis  = 1,
   340→                      add_sum = FALSE,
   341→                      label   = "trial") {
   342→
   343→  # Create an expression that will evaluate .trial_factor(length(onsets)) 
   344→  # when the onsets variable is available at evaluation time
   345→  trial_expr <- rlang::expr(.trial_factor(length(onsets)))
   346→
   347→  term <- hrf(!!trial_expr,  # Use !! to inject the expression
   348→              basis = basis,
   349→              lag   = lag,
   350→              nbasis = nbasis,
   351→              id     = label) # Use id argument for naming
   352→
   353→  term$add_sum <- isTRUE(add_sum) # flag for construct() to act upon
   354→  term
   355→}
   356→
   357→#' Internal helper for generating trial factors
   358→#'
   359→#' @param n Length of the factor to generate.
   360→#' @keywords internal
   361→.trial_factor <- function(n) {
   362→  pad <- nchar(as.character(n))
   363→  factor(sprintf(paste0("%0", pad, "d"), seq_len(n)))
   364→}

#' @importFrom rlang enquos enexpr syms is_formula is_quosure is_call as_label %||% quo_get_expr is_symbol
#' @importFrom assertthat assert_that
#' @importFrom stats setNames


#' @keywords internal
#' @noRd
make_hrf <- function(basis, lag, nbasis=1) {
  if (!is.numeric(lag) || length(lag) > 1) {
    stop("hrf: 'lag' must be a numeric scalar")
  }
  
  if (is.character(basis)) {
    # Map character names to HRF constants
    hrf_map <- list(
      "spmg1" = fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1,
      "spmg2" = fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG2,
      "spmg3" = fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG3,
      "gamma" = fmrihrf::HRF_GAMMA,
      "gaussian" = fmrihrf::HRF_GAUSSIAN,
      "bspline" = fmrihrf::HRF_BSPLINE,
      "fir" = fmrihrf::HRF_FIR
    )
    
    if (basis %in% names(hrf_map)) {
      base_hrf_obj <- hrf_map[[basis]]
      # Check if nbasis needs to be customized for bases that support it
      if (basis == "bspline" && nbasis != fmrihrf::nbasis(base_hrf_obj)) {
        # Create custom bspline HRF function with specified nbasis
        span <- attr(base_hrf_obj, "span")
        if (is.null(span)) span <- 24
        degree <- attr(base_hrf_obj, "params")$degree
        if (is.null(degree)) degree <- 3
        custom_bspline_fn <- function(t) {
          fmrihrf::hrf_bspline(t, span = span, N = nbasis, degree = degree)
        }
        base_hrf_obj <- fmrihrf::as_hrf(custom_bspline_fn, name = "bspline", nbasis = nbasis, span = span)
      } else if (basis == "fir" && nbasis != fmrihrf::nbasis(base_hrf_obj)) {
        # Create custom FIR HRF with specified nbasis
        span <- attr(base_hrf_obj, "span")
        if (is.null(span)) span <- 24
        custom_fir_fn <- function(t) {
          fmrihrf:::hrf_tent_generator(span = span, nbasis = nbasis)(t)
        }
                base_hrf_obj <- fmrihrf::as_hrf(custom_fir_fn, name = "fir", nbasis = nbasis, span = span)
      }
    } else if (basis == "fourier") {
      # Create Fourier HRF with specified nbasis
      span <- 24  # Default span for Fourier
      custom_fourier_fn <- function(t) {
        fmrihrf::hrf_fourier(t, span = span, nbasis = nbasis)
      }
      base_hrf_obj <- fmrihrf::as_hrf(custom_fourier_fn, name = "fourier", nbasis = nbasis, span = span)
    } else {
      # For unknown basis names, just use the default (this will likely fail, but matches old behavior)
      stop("Unknown HRF basis name: ", basis, ". Available options: ", paste(c(names(hrf_map), "fourier"), collapse = ", "))
    }
    # Apply lag using gen_hrf
    final_hrf <- fmrihrf::gen_hrf(base_hrf_obj, lag = lag)

  } else if (inherits(basis, "HRF")) {
    # If it's already an HRF object, apply lag using gen_hrf
    final_hrf <- fmrihrf::gen_hrf(basis, lag = lag)
    
  } else if (is.function(basis)) {
    # If it's a raw function, gen_hrf will handle conversion via as_hrf and apply lag
    final_hrf <- fmrihrf::gen_hrf(basis, lag = lag)

  } else {
    stop("invalid basis function: must be 1) character string indicating hrf type, e.g. 'gamma' 2) a function or 3) an object of class 'HRF': ", basis)
  }
  
  return(final_hrf)
}

#' hemodynamic regressor specification function for model formulas.
#' 
#' This function is to be used in formulas for fitting functions, e.g. onsets ~ hrf(fac1,fac2) ...
#' It captures the variables/expressions provided and packages them with HRF/contrast 
#' information into an `hrfspec` object, which is then processed by `event_model`.
#' 
#' @param ... One or more variable names (bare or character) or expressions involving variables 
#'            present in the `data` argument of `event_model`.
#' @param basis the impulse response function or the name of a pre-supplied function, 
#'        one of: "gamma", "spmg1", "spmg2", "spmg3", "bspline", "gaussian", "tent", "bs". 
#'        Can also be an `HRF` object.
#' @param onsets optional onsets override. If missing, onsets will be taken from the LHS of the main model formula.
#' @param durations optional durations override. If missing, durations argument from `event_model` is used.
#' @param prefix a character string that is prepended to the variable names and used to identify the term. 
#'               Can be used to disambiguate two \code{hrf} terms with the same variable(s) but different onsets or basis functions.
#' @param subset an expression indicating the subset of 'onsets' to keep.
#' @param precision sampling precision in seconds.
#' @param nbasis number of basis functions -- only used for hemodynamic response functions (e.g. bspline) that take a variable number of bases.
#' @param contrasts one or more \code{contrast_spec} objects created with the \code{contrast}, `pair_contrast` etc. functions. 
#'                  Must be NULL, a single contrast spec, or a *named* list of contrast specs.
#' @param id a unique \code{character} identifier used to refer to term, otherwise will be determined from variable names.
#' @param name Optional human-readable name for the term.
#' @param lag a temporal offset in seconds which is added to onset before convolution
#' @param summate whether impulse amplitudes sum up when duration is greater than 0.
#' @examples 
#' 
#' ## 'hrf' is typically used in the context of \code{formula}s passed to `event_model`.
#' 
#' # Simple model with one factor
#' form1 <- onsets ~ hrf(condition, basis="spmg1")
#' 
#' # Model with factor and continuous modulator, using default SPMG1 for both terms
#' form2 <- onsets ~ hrf(condition) + hrf(RT)
#' 
#' # Model with interaction term and SPMG3 basis
#' form3 <- onsets ~ hrf(condition, RT, basis="spmg3")
#' 
#' # Model with an expression and contrasts
#' library(rlang)
#' con1 <- pair_contrast(~ condition == "A", ~ condition == "B", name="AvB")
#' form4 <- onsets ~ hrf(condition, Poly(RT, 2), contrasts=con1)
#' 
#' @export
#' @importFrom rlang enquos enexpr syms is_formula is_quosure is_call as_label %||%
#' @return an \code{hrfspec} instance
hrf <- function(..., basis="spmg1", onsets=NULL, durations=NULL, prefix=NULL, subset=NULL, precision=.3, 
                nbasis=1, contrasts=NULL, id=NULL, name=NULL, lag=0, summate=TRUE) {
  
  vars <- rlang::enquos(...) # Capture variables/expressions as quosures
  
  # --- Handle special named arguments within ... ---
  var_names <- names(vars)
  # Remove any quosures whose name matches hrf formals (these are control args, not vars)
  hrf_formals <- names(formals(hrf))
  var_indices <- ! (var_names %in% hrf_formals)
  vars <- vars[var_indices]
  
  if (length(vars) == 0) {
      stop("`hrf` must have at least one variable or expression specified in `...`")
  }
  
  # --- Determine Term ID/Name (Prioritize id, then name, then auto) --- 
  final_id <- id # Prioritize explicit id
  if (is.null(final_id) && !is.null(name)) { # Use name if id is missing
      final_id <- name
  }
  # final_id remains NULL if neither id nor name provided, hrfspec will generate default
  
  # --- Check contrasts argument --- 
  if (!is.null(contrasts)) {
    if (!is.list(contrasts)) {
        if (inherits(contrasts, "contrast_spec")) {
            contrast_list <- list(contrasts)
            cname <- contrasts$name %||% "contrast1"
            names(contrast_list) <- cname
            contrasts <- contrast_list
        } else {
             stop("`contrasts=` argument must be a single contrast_spec object or a list of them.\n",
                  "  Detected type: ", class(contrasts)[1], "\n",
                  "  Hint: Use functions like contrast(), pair_contrast(), contrast_set() to define contrasts.", 
                  call. = FALSE)
        }
    } else {
        is_spec <- vapply(contrasts, inherits, logical(1), "contrast_spec")
        if (!all(is_spec)) {
             stop("If `contrasts=` is a list, all elements must be contrast_spec objects.\n",
                  "  Hint: Use functions like contrast(), pair_contrast(), contrast_set() to define contrasts.", 
                  call. = FALSE)
        }
        if (is.null(names(contrasts)) || any(names(contrasts) == "")) {
            cnames <- sapply(contrasts, function(cs) cs$name %||% paste0("contrast", which(sapply(contrasts, identical, cs))))
             if (any(duplicated(cnames))) {
                stop("If `contrasts=` is a list, it must be a *named* list (or auto-naming must yield unique names).", call. = FALSE)
            }
            names(contrasts) <- cnames
        }
    }
  }
  # -----------------------------
  
  basis_obj <- make_hrf(basis, lag, nbasis=nbasis)
  
  # Call the internal constructor, passing quosures directly
  ret <- hrfspec(
    vars = vars, # Pass list of quosures captured by enquos
    basis = basis_obj,         
    onsets = onsets,       
    durations = durations, 
    prefix = prefix,       
    subset = rlang::enexpr(subset), # Capture subset expr unevaluated
    precision = precision,
    contrasts = contrasts, ## Pass validated list of contrast specs
    summate = summate,
    id = final_id # Pass the determined ID 
    )

  ret
}


#' Internal constructor for hrfspec objects
#' 
#' Creates the hrfspec list structure. Called by `hrf()`.
#' Generates termname and label from the input variables/expressions.
#'
#' @param vars List of quosures representing variables/expressions.
#' @param label Optional label for the term (if NULL, generated automatically).
#' @param basis An `HRF` object.
#' @param ... Other arguments passed from `hrf()` (onsets, durations, prefix, subset, etc.)
#' @return An `hrfspec` instance (list with class `hrfspec`).
#' @importFrom rlang as_label is_symbol is_call quo_get_expr
#' @noRd
#' @keywords internal
hrfspec <- function(vars, label=NULL, basis=fmrihrf::HRF_SPMG1, ...) {

  assert_that(inherits(basis, "HRF"))
  
  # Generate varnames and termname from quosures
  var_labels <- sapply(vars, rlang::as_label)
  # Simple heuristic for names: use symbol directly, otherwise make.names on label
  varnames <- sapply(vars, function(q) {
       expr <- rlang::quo_get_expr(q)
       if (rlang::is_symbol(expr)) as.character(expr) else make.names(rlang::as_label(expr))
  })
  
  termname <- paste0(varnames, collapse=":")
  
  # Generate label if not provided
  if (is.null(label)) {
      label <- paste0("hrf(", paste0(var_labels, collapse=","), ")")
  }
  
  # Capture other arguments passed via ... from hrf()
  other_args <- list(...)
  
  ret <- list(
    name = termname, 
    label = label,
    id = other_args$id,
    vars = vars,
    varnames = varnames, 
    hrf = basis,
    onsets = other_args$onsets,
    durations = other_args$durations,
    prefix = other_args$prefix,
    subset = other_args$subset, # Should be an expression
    precision = other_args$precision %||% 0.3,
    contrasts = other_args$contrasts, # Already validated list or NULL
    summate = other_args$summate %||% TRUE
    # data_env is NOT stored here, added later during evaluation
  )
  
  # If user supplied an explicit id (or via name= alias), use it as the primary term name
  if (!is.null(other_args$id)) {
      ret$name <- other_args$id
  }
  
  class(ret) <- c("hrfspec", "list")
  ret
}



#' Get number of basis functions from hrfspec
#'
#' @param x An hrfspec object
#' @param ... Additional arguments (unused)
#' @return The number of basis functions
#' @export
nbasis.hrfspec <- function(x, ...) {
  fmrihrf::nbasis(x$hrf)
}

#' Retrieve contrast specifications from an hrfspec
#'
#' @param x An `hrfspec` object.
#' @param ... Unused.
#' @return The list of contrast specifications attached to the hrfspec, or `NULL`.
#' @export
contrasts.hrfspec <- function(x, ...) {
  x$contrasts
}

#' @export
construct.hrfspec <- function(x, model_spec, ...) {
  ons <- if (!is.null(x$onsets)) x$onsets else model_spec$onsets
  et <- construct_event_term(x, model_spec)
  
  # Set the term_tag attribute on the event_term before returning
  # This ensures that when convolution happens later, the correct column names are generated
  term_tag <- x$id %||% x$name
  attr(et, "term_tag") <- term_tag
  
  # DON'T convolve here - let build_event_model_design_matrix handle convolution
  # Just return the event_term with hrfspec attached
  # The hrfspec is already attached in construct_event_term
  
  # Handle add_sum flag if present in the hrfspec (set by trialwise)
  # Store this as an attribute for later processing during convolution
  if (isTRUE(x$add_sum)) {
    attr(et, "add_sum") <- TRUE
    attr(et, "add_sum_label") <- x$id %||% x$name
  }

  # Return the event_term directly, not a convolved_term
  et
}

#' evaluate.hrfspec
#'
#' This function evaluates a hemodynamic response function (HRF) specified by an hrfspec object for a given set of time points (grid) and other parameters.
#' It is a wrapper function that calls the evaluate.HRF function with the HRF function contained in the hrfspec object.
#'
#' @param x The hrfspec object containing the HRF function.
#' @param grid A vector of time points.
#' @param amplitude The scaling value for the event (default: 1).
#' @param duration The duration of the event (default: 0).
#' @param precision The temporal resolution used for computing summed responses when duration > 0 (default: 0.1).
#' @param ... Additional arguments to be passed to the evaluate.HRF function.
#' @return A vector of HRF values at the specified time points.
#' @noRd
evaluate.hrfspec <- function(x, grid, amplitude=1, duration=0, precision=.1, ...) {
  fmrihrf::evaluate(x$hrf, grid, amplitude, duration, precision)
}

#' trialwise
#'
#' Generate one regressor per trial (plus an optional grand-mean column)
#' by delegating everything to `hrf()`.
#'
#' Use it **only on the RHS** of an event-model formula:
#'
#'     onset ~ trialwise(basis = "spmg1", add_sum = TRUE)
#'
#' @param basis,lag,nbasis Passed straight to `hrf()`.
#' @param add_sum If TRUE, append a column that is the average of all
#'                trialwise columns (useful as a conventional main effect).
#' @param label Term label / prefix for the generated columns.
#' @export
trialwise <- function(basis   = "spmg1",
                      lag     = 0,
                      nbasis  = 1,
                      add_sum = FALSE,
                      label   = "trial") {

  # Create an expression that will evaluate .trial_factor(length(onsets)) 
  # when the onsets variable is available at evaluation time
  trial_expr <- rlang::expr(.trial_factor(length(onsets)))

  term <- hrf(!!trial_expr,  # Use !! to inject the expression
              basis = basis,
              lag   = lag,
              nbasis = nbasis,
              id     = label) # Use id argument for naming

  term$add_sum <- isTRUE(add_sum) # flag for construct() to act upon
  term
}

#' Internal helper for generating trial factors
#'
#' @param n Length of the factor to generate.
#' @keywords internal
.trial_factor <- function(n) {
  pad <- nchar(as.character(n))
  factor(sprintf(paste0("%0", pad, "d"), seq_len(n)))
}

1→###############################################################################
     2→# EVENT_VECTOR.R
     3→#
     4→# This file contains helper routines for cleaning names, checking ordering,
     5→# constructing event model terms (and various event types), extracting design
     6→# matrices, computing contrasts, and printing event-related objects for fMRI.
     7→#
     8→###############################################################################
     9→
    10→## ============================================================================
    11→## Section 1: Helper Functions (Moved to R/utils-internal.R)
    12→## ============================================================================
    13→
    14→# Removed .sanitizeName
    15→# Removed is.increasing
    16→# Removed is.strictly.increasing
    17→# Removed .checkEVArgs
    18→
    19→## ============================================================================
    20→## Section 2: Event Term Construction
    21→## ============================================================================
    22→
    23→#' Create an event model term from a named list of variables.
    24→#'
    25→#' Generates an `event_term` object which represents the combination of one 
    26→#' or more event sequences (e.g., a factor crossed with a numeric modulator).
    27→#' It takes a list of variables (factors, numeric vectors, matrices, basis objects)
    28→#' along with shared onsets, block IDs, and durations.
    29→#' It uses the `EV` factory internally to create standardized `event` objects for each variable.
    30→#'
    31→#' @param evlist A named list of variables (factors, numeric, matrices, ParametricBasis objects).
    32→#'        The names are used as variable identifiers within the term.
    33→#' @param onsets Numeric vector of onset times (in seconds).
    34→#' @param blockids Numeric vector of block numbers (non-decreasing integers).
    35→#' @param durations Numeric vector of event durations (seconds, default is 0). 
    36→#'        Can be scalar (recycled) or vector matching length of `onsets`.
    37→#' @param subset Optional logical vector indicating which events to retain (applied before processing).
    38→#'
    39→#' @return A list object with class `c("event_term", "event_seq")`. Contains:
    40→#'   \item{varname}{Concatenated variable names from `evlist`.} 
    41→#'   \item{events}{A named list of the processed `event` objects.} 
    42→#'   \item{subset}{The `subset` vector used.} 
    43→#'   \item{event_table}{A tibble representing the combinations of descriptive levels/names 
    44→#'                    for each event in the term, constructed using `elements(..., values=FALSE)`.} 
    45→#'   \item{onsets}{Numeric vector of onsets (after processing/subsetting).} 
    46→#'   \item{blockids}{Numeric vector of block IDs (after processing/subsetting).} 
    47→#'   \item{durations}{Numeric vector of durations (after processing/subsetting).} 
    48→#'
    49→#' @examples 
    50→#' x1 <- factor(rep(letters[1:3], 10))
    51→#' x2 <- factor(rep(1:3, each = 10))
    52→#' onsets <- seq(1, 100, length.out = 30)
    53→#' blockids <- rep(1:3, each = 10)
    54→#' 
    55→#' eterm <- event_term(list(Condition = x1, Group = x2),
    56→#'                     onsets = onsets,
    57→#'                     blockids = blockids)
    58→#' print(eterm)
    59→#' head(event_table(eterm))
    60→#' levels(eterm)
    61→#' head(design_matrix(eterm))
    62→#'
    63→#' @export
    64→#' @import assertthat
    65→#' @importFrom tibble as_tibble tibble
    66→event_term <- function(evlist, onsets, blockids, durations = 0, subset = NULL) {
    67→  
    68→  # Convert blockids to numeric if they are factors.
    69→  if (is.factor(blockids)) {
    70→    blockids <- as.numeric(as.character(blockids))
    71→  }
    72→  
    73→  # Ensure blockids are non-decreasing.
    74→  # Use base R equivalent of is.increasing
    75→  assert_that(!is.unsorted(blockids), msg = "'blockids' must consist of non-decreasing integers")
    76→  
    77→  # NOTE: subset handling is now primarily inside event(), 
    78→  # but event_term might still need its own subset logic if it uses it before calling EV?
    79→  # Currently, it passes subset down to EV.
    80→  if (is.null(subset)) { 
    81→    subset <- rep(TRUE, length(onsets)) 
    82→  }
    83→  
    84→  # Duration recycling is handled inside event() via .checkEVArgs
    85→  # if (length(durations) == 1) {
    86→  #   durations <- rep(durations, length(onsets))
    87→  # }
    88→  
    89→  vnames <- names(evlist)
    90→  onlen <- length(onsets)
    91→  
    92→  # Basic check on input lengths before calling EV factory
    93→  getlen <- function(v) {
    94→    if (inherits(v, "event")) length(v$onsets)
    95→    else if (is.matrix(v)) nrow(v)
    96→    else if (inherits(v, "ParametricBasis")) nrow(v$y) # Check basis matrix dim
    97→    else length(v)
    98→  }
    99→  for(i in seq_along(evlist)) {
   100→      assert_that(getlen(evlist[[i]]) == onlen, 
   101→                  msg=sprintf("Length mismatch between onsets (%d) and variable '%s' (%d)", 
   102→                              onlen, vnames[i], getlen(evlist[[i]])))
   103→  }
   104→  
   105→  # Create event objects by dispatching to the appropriate public wrapper
   106→  evs <- lapply(seq_along(evlist), function(i) {
   107→    vals <- evlist[[i]]
   108→    vname_i <- vnames[i]
   109→    
   110→    # Type checking and dispatching (replaces EV factory logic)
   111→    if (inherits(vals, "event")) {
   112→        # If it's already an event object, just use it as-is
   113→        # TODO: Consider whether to re-apply subset/durations/etc from current call
   114→        vals
   115→    } else if (inherits(vals, "ParametricBasis")) {
   116→        event_basis(basis = vals, name = vname_i, onsets = onsets, blockids = blockids, durations = durations, subset = subset)
   117→    } else if (is.factor(vals) || is.character(vals)) {
   118→        event_factor(fac = vals, name = vname_i, onsets = onsets, blockids = blockids, durations = durations, subset = subset)
   119→    } else if (is.matrix(vals)) {
   120→        # Handle single-column matrices as vectors for event_variable?
   121→        # No, event_matrix handles matrices directly now.
   122→        event_matrix(mat = vals, name = vname_i, onsets = onsets, blockids = blockids, durations = durations, subset = subset)
   123→    } else if (is.numeric(vals) && (is.vector(vals) || length(dim(vals)) <= 1)) {
   124→         # Check specifically for numeric vectors (or 1D arrays)
   125→        event_variable(vec = vals, name = vname_i, onsets = onsets, blockids = blockids, durations = durations, subset = subset)
   126→    } else {
   127→        stop(sprintf("Unsupported value type '%s' for variable '%s' in event_term", class(vals)[1], vname_i))
   128→    }
   129→  })
   130→  
   131→  names(evs) <- sapply(evs, function(ev) ev$varname)
   132→  pterms <- unlist(lapply(evs, function(ev) ev$varname))
   133→  
   134→  # Check if any event object creation resulted in zero events after subsetting
   135→  if (length(evs) > 0 && length(evs[[1]]$onsets) == 0) {
   136→      warning(sprintf("Event term '%s' resulted in zero events after subsetting/processing.", 
   137→                      paste(vnames, collapse=":")))
   138→      # Return an empty structure? Or let downstream fail? Let downstream handle for now.
   139→      # It should have empty onsets, durations, blockids, value matrix. 
   140→  }
   141→  
   142→  # Rebuild event_table based on the *actual* content of the event objects
   143→  # event() now handles the internal structure, so we use elements()
   144→  # This relies on elements.event(..., values=FALSE) returning the appropriate factor levels/names
   145→  
   146→  # Get descriptive elements (levels/names) for each event
   147→  descriptive_elements <- elements(evs[[1]], values = FALSE) # Need a way to call elements on list 'evs'? 
   148→  # No, elements.event_term works on the term object after it's built.
   149→  # Let's reconstruct the table *after* building the initial list. 
   150→
   151→  varname <- paste(sapply(evs, function(x) x$varname), collapse = ":")
   152→  
   153→  # Create the list structure first
   154→  ret <- list(varname = varname, 
   155→              events = evs, 
   156→              subset = subset, # Keep original subset for reference?
   157→              # Placeholder for event_table, rebuild below
   158→              event_table = NULL, 
   159→              # Use onsets/blockids/durations from the *first* processed event object
   160→              # Assumes they are consistent across all events after processing (checked by event())
   161→              onsets = if(length(evs) > 0) evs[[1]]$onsets else numeric(0), 
   162→              blockids = if(length(evs) > 0) evs[[1]]$blockids else numeric(0), 
   163→              durations = if(length(evs) > 0) evs[[1]]$durations else numeric(0))
   164→  
   165→  class(ret) <- c("event_term", "event_seq")
   166→  
   167→  # Now build the event_table using elements()
   168→  # Get descriptive elements (levels/names) for the term
   169→  descriptive_elements_list <- elements(ret, what = "labels") # Explicitly request labels
   170→  # Combine into a tibble directly from the list
   171→  etab <- try(tibble::as_tibble(descriptive_elements_list), silent = TRUE)
   172→  if (inherits(etab, "try-error")) {
   173→      warning("Failed to create event_table for term: ", varname)
   174→      etab <- tibble::tibble()
   175→  }
   176→  ret$event_table <- etab
   177→  
   178→  ret
   179→}
   180→
   181→#' @export
   182→event_table.event_term <- function(x, ...) x$event_table
   183→
   184→## ============================================================================
   185→## Section 3: EV Factory and Event Constructors (REMOVE EV Factory)
   186→## ============================================================================
   187→
   188→# Removed EV factory function. Logic is now inlined in event_term.
   189→
   190→# Removed event_factor, event_variable, event_matrix, event_basis wrappers.
   191→# They are now located in R/event-classes.R
   192→
   193→## ============================================================================
   194→## Section 4: Levels and Formula Methods
   195→## ============================================================================
   196→
   197→#' @method formula event_term
   198→#' @export
   199→formula.event_term <- function(x, ...) {
   200→  # NOTE: This uses parent_terms.event_term which still exists below
   201→  #       It might need adjustment if parent_terms logic changes.
   202→  as.formula(paste("~ (", paste(parent_terms(x), collapse = ":"), "-1)"))
   203→}
   204→
   205→#' @noRd
   206→.vector_of_labels <- function(ev) {
   207→  # Helper to generate the vector of condition labels for a single event.
   208→  # Uses levels.event() for the actual levels/column names.
   209→  lvls <- levels(ev) # Get levels/colnames from levels.event
   210→  
   211→  if (is_continuous(ev) && length(lvls) > 1) {
   212→    # Continuous multi-column (matrix/basis): Use index 1:ncol
   213→    vapply(seq_along(lvls), 
   214→           \(k) .label_component(ev, k), 
   215→           character(1))
   216→  } else if (is_categorical(ev)) {
   217→    # Categorical: Use actual levels
   218→    vapply(lvls, \(lvl) .label_component(ev, lvl), character(1))
   219→  } else {
   220→    # Single continuous variable (vector): Just the variable name
   221→    .label_component(ev)
   222→  }
   223→}

   225→## ============================================================================
   226→## Section 5: Cells Extraction
   227→## ============================================================================
   228→
   229→#' @method cells event_term
   230→#' @rdname cells
   231→#' @export
   232→cells.event_term <- function(x, drop.empty = TRUE, ...) {
   233→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
   234→  ## 0. fast cache ---------------------------------------------------
   235→  # Use fixed name as levels rarely change post-construction
   236→  cache_attr_name <- "..cells" 
   237→  if (!is.null(cached <- attr(x, cache_attr_name))) {
   238→    cnt <- attr(cached, "count")
   239→    # Need to handle potential NULL count if cache is invalid
   240→    if(is.null(cnt)) {
   241→        warning("Invalid cache detected for cells.event_term, recomputing.")
   242→    } else {
   243→        return(if (drop.empty) cached[cnt > 0, , drop = FALSE] else cached)
   244→    }
   245→  }
   246→
   247→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
   248→  ## 1. categorical events only -------------------------------------
   249→  # Use Filter and Negate for conciseness
   250→  cats <- Filter(Negate(is_continuous), x$events)
   251→
   252→  if (length(cats) == 0) {                  # no factors => one big cell
   253→    # Use a more descriptive name if needed, maybe based on varname
   254→    # Consistent with cells.event: use the (first/only) varname
   255→    var_name_cont <- if (length(x$events) > 0) x$events[[1]]$varname else "all_events"
   256→    out <- tibble::tibble(!!var_name_cont := var_name_cont) # Use varname for column
   257→    count_val <- length(x$onsets)
   258→    attr(out, "count") <- count_val
   259→    # Assign name to the count attribute
   260→    names(attr(out, "count")) <- var_name_cont 
   261→    
   262→    attr(x, cache_attr_name) <- out # Cache the result
   263→    return(out)
   264→  }
   265→
   266→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
   267→  ## 2. observed combination counts ---------------------------------
   268→  # Reconstruct factors from internal representation
   269→  obs_list <- lapply(cats, \(ev) {
   270→      # Add checks for valid meta$levels and value structure
   271→      if (is.null(ev$meta$levels) || !is.matrix(ev$value) || ncol(ev$value) != 1) {
   272→           stop(paste("Invalid internal structure for categorical event:", ev$varname), call.=FALSE)
   273→      }
   274→      factor(ev$value[, 1L], levels = seq_along(ev$meta$levels), labels = ev$meta$levels)
   275→  })
   276→  # Create data frame of observed factor combinations
   277→  obs <- do.call(data.frame, c(obs_list, stringsAsFactors = FALSE))
   278→  
   279→  # Use table() for efficient contingency counting (if few factors)
   280→  # For many factors, alternative might be needed 
   281→  # tbl <- as.data.frame.matrix(table(obs)) # This creates wide format, not needed directly
   282→  
   283→  # Generate the grid of all possible level combinations
   284→  levels_list <- lapply(cats, levels)
   285→  grid <- expand.grid(levels_list, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) # Use FALSE then convert relevant columns
   286→  # Ensure column names match original variable names
   287→  colnames(grid) <- names(cats)
   288→  # Convert grid columns to factors matching the levels in obs_list
   289→  for(i in seq_along(grid)){
   290→      grid[[i]] <- factor(grid[[i]], levels=levels(obs_list[[i]]))
   291→  }
   292→
   293→  ## match() much faster than join for simple cases ------------------
   294→  # Create unique string keys for observed and grid rows
   295→  # Use sep that's unlikely to appear in levels
   296→  key_obs  <- do.call(paste, c(obs, sep = "\001")) 
   297→  key_grid <- do.call(paste, c(grid, sep = "\001"))
   298→  # Count occurrences by matching observed keys to grid keys
   299→  count    <- tabulate(match(key_obs, key_grid), nbins = nrow(grid))
   300→
   301→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
   302→  ## 3. assemble result ---------------------------------------------
   303→  out <- tibble::as_tibble(grid) # Convert final grid to tibble
   304→
   305→  # --- Add names to the count vector ---
   306→  # Generate names for the counts based on the grid rows (factor level combinations)
   307→  if (nrow(grid) > 0) {
   308→      # Use a separator consistent with how interactions might be named elsewhere
   309→      cell_names <- apply(grid, 1, paste, collapse = ":")
   310→      # Ensure counts has names before attaching
   311→      if (length(count) == length(cell_names)) {
   312→         names(count) <- cell_names
   313→      } else {
   314→         # This shouldn't happen if logic above is correct, but add a warning
   315→         warning("Mismatch between number of cells and counts calculated in cells.event_term")
   316→      }
   317→  } # Else count is likely integer(0) and doesn't need names
   318→  # --- End adding names ---
   319→
   320→  attr(out, "count") <- count # Attach the now named count vector
   321→
   322→  attr(x, cache_attr_name) <- out # Cache the result (with named counts)
   323→
   324→  # Filter based on drop.empty
   325→  if (drop.empty) {
   326→      keep_idx <- count > 0
   327→      filtered_out <- out[keep_idx, , drop = FALSE]
   328→      # Ensure the count attribute on the filtered output is also correct
   329→      attr(filtered_out, "count") <- count[keep_idx]
   330→      filtered_out
   331→  } else {
   332→       out
   333→  }
   334→}
   335→
   336→#' @noRd
   337→.event_set <- function(x, exclude_basis = FALSE) {
   338→  evtab <- event_table(x)
   339→  
   340→  evset <- if (fmrihrf::nbasis(x) > 1 & !exclude_basis) {
   341→    ncond <- fmrihrf::nbasis(x)
   342→    # Construct a zero-padded string for basis labels.
   343→    zstr <- paste0(rep("0", ceiling(log10(ncond + 1e-6))), collapse = "")
   344→    
   345→          evlist <- c(list(factor(paste("basis", zstr, 1:fmrihrf::nbasis(x), sep = ""))), cells(x$evterm))
   346→    names(evlist) <- c("basis", parent_terms(x$evterm))
   347→    evlist <- lapply(evlist, levels)
   348→    ret <- expand.grid(evlist, stringsAsFactors = TRUE)
   349→    ret[c(2:length(ret), 1)]
   350→  } else {
   351→    cells(x$evterm)
   352→  }
   353→}

   357→cells.covariate_convolved_term <- function(x, ...) {

   361→#' @export
   362→#' @importFrom stringr str_trim
   363→cells.convolved_term <- function(x, ...) {
   364→  exclude_basis <- list(...)$exclude_basis
   365→  if (is.null(exclude_basis)) exclude_basis <- FALSE
   366→  
   367→  evtab <- event_table(x)
   368→  evset <- .event_set(x, exclude_basis = exclude_basis)
   369→  
   370→  strels <- apply(apply(evtab, 2, stringr::str_trim), 1, paste, collapse = ":")
   371→  
   372→  strlevs <- if (nrow(evset) > 1) {
   373→    apply(apply(evset, 2, stringr::str_trim), 1, paste, collapse = ":")
   374→  } else {
   375→    as.character(evset[1, 1])
   376→  }
   377→  
   378→  attr(evset, "rownames") <- strlevs
   379→  
   380→  counts <- if (exclude_basis) {
   381→    rep(attr(cells(x$evterm), "count"), each = 1)
   382→  } else {
   383→          rep(attr(cells(x$evterm), "count"), each = fmrihrf::nbasis(x))
   384→  }
   385→  
   386→  ret <- evset[counts > 0, , drop = FALSE]
   387→  attr(ret, "count") <- counts[counts > 0]
   388→  ret
   389→}

   391→## ============================================================================
   392→## Section 6: Conditions and Parent Terms
   393→## ============================================================================
   394→
   395→#' @method conditions event_term
   396→#' @rdname conditions
   397→#' @export
   398→#' @importFrom tibble tibble
   399→#' @importFrom rlang :=
   400→conditions.event_term <- function(x, drop.empty = TRUE, expand_basis = FALSE, ...) {
   401→  
   402→  # --- Caching --- 
   403→  opts_key <- paste(drop.empty, expand_basis, sep="|") # Keep drop.empty in key for now
   404→  cached_val <- attr(x, "..conds")
   405→  cached_opts <- attr(x, "..conds_opts")
   406→  
   407→  # --- RE-ENABLE CACHE --- 
   408→  if (!is.null(cached_val) && !is.null(cached_opts) && identical(cached_opts, opts_key)) {
   409→    return(cached_val)
   410→  }
   411→  # message("--- conditions.event_term: Cache bypassed/missed, recalculating ---") # Keep commented out
   412→  # --- END RE-ENABLE ---
   413→  
   414→  # --- Shortcut for single continuous event with one column --- 
   415→  if (length(x$events) == 1 && is_continuous(x$events[[1]])) {
   416→    cols <- try(columns(x$events[[1]]), silent=TRUE)
   417→    if (!inherits(cols, "try-error") && length(cols) == 1) {
   418→        base_cond_tags <- cols 
   419→        if (expand_basis) {
   420→            hrfspec <- attr(x, "hrfspec")
   421→            nb <- if (!is.null(hrfspec) && !is.null(hrfspec$hrf)) fmrihrf::nbasis(hrfspec$hrf) else 1L
   422→            final_cond_tags <- add_basis(base_cond_tags, nb)
   423→        } else {
   424→            final_cond_tags <- base_cond_tags
   425→        }
   426→        attr(x, "..conds") <- final_cond_tags
   427→        attr(x, "..conds_opts") <- opts_key
   428→        return(final_cond_tags)
   429→    }
   430→  }
   431→  
   432→  # --- Generate Tokens for Each Component --- 
   433→  comp_tokens_list <- lapply(x$events, function(ev) {
   434→      if (is_categorical(ev)) {
   435→          levs <- levels(ev) 
   436→          # Handle case where factor might have no levels after subsetting? levels() should return character(0)
   437→          if (length(levs) == 0) return(character(0))
   438→          level_token(ev$varname, levs)
   439→      } else {
   440→          columns(ev) 
   441→      }
   442→  })
   443→  
   444→  # Filter out components that returned empty tokens (e.g., factors with no levels)
   445→  comp_tokens_list <- Filter(function(tk) length(tk) > 0, comp_tokens_list)
   446→  
   447→  if (length(comp_tokens_list) == 0) { # If ALL components became empty
   448→       final_out <- character(0)
   449→       attr(x, "..conds") <- final_out
   450→       attr(x, "..conds_opts") <- opts_key
   451→       return(final_out)
   452→  }
   453→  
   454→  # --- Combine Tokens using expand.grid and make_cond_tag --- 
   455→  names(comp_tokens_list) <- names(Filter(function(tk) length(tk) > 0, x$events)) # Match names to filtered tokens
   456→  full_grid <- expand.grid(comp_tokens_list, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
   457→  base_cond_tags_all <- apply(full_grid, 1, make_cond_tag)
   458→  
   459→  # --- REMOVED drop.empty LOGIC BLOCK --- 
   460→  # The logic relying on cells() was flawed for mixed continuous/categorical terms.
   461→  # Dropping based on actual matrix rank deficiency is handled by design_matrix() / model.matrix().
   462→  base_cond_tags_final <- base_cond_tags_all
   463→  
   464→  # --- Handle expand_basis --- 
   465→  if (expand_basis) {
   466→      hrfspec <- attr(x, "hrfspec")
   467→      nb <- if (!is.null(hrfspec) && !is.null(hrfspec$hrf)) fmrihrf::nbasis(hrfspec$hrf) else 1L
   468→      final_cond_tags <- add_basis(base_cond_tags_final, nb)
   469→  } else {
   470→      final_cond_tags <- base_cond_tags_final
   471→  }
   472→  
   473→  # --- Cache and Return --- 
   474→  final_out <- as.vector(final_cond_tags)
   475→  attr(x, "..conds") <- final_out
   476→  attr(x, "..conds_opts") <- opts_key
   477→  
   478→  return(final_out)
   479→}

   520→#' @method elements event_term

   522→#' @export
   523→elements.event_term <- function(x, what = c("values", "labels"), ...) {
   524→  # Ensure 'what' is determined correctly, respecting 'values' if passed via ...
   525→  dots <- list(...)
   526→  if (!missing(what)) {
   527→      what <- match.arg(what)
   528→  } else if (!is.null(dots$values)) {
   529→      what <- if (dots$values) "values" else "labels"
   530→  } else {
   531→      what <- "values" # Default if neither 'what' nor 'values' is specified
   532→  }
   533→  
   534→  # Pass the determined 'what' argument and any other arguments down
   535→  # lapply will now create a named list directly as elements.event returns vectors/matrices
   536→  els <- lapply(x$events, elements, what = what, ...)
   537→  
   538→  # Sanitize names of the resulting list (which should already be named by lapply)
   539→  # If lapply didn't preserve names from x$events, this is needed.
   540→  names(els) <- vapply(names(x$events), .sanitizeName, character(1))
   541→  els
   542→}

   571→
   572→#' @method split_onsets event_term
   573→#' @rdname split_onsets
   574→#' @export
   575→split_onsets.event_term <- function(x, sframe, global = FALSE, blocksplit = FALSE, ...) {
   576→  # Get categorical events.
   577→  facs <- x$events[!sapply(x$events, is_continuous)]
   578→  
   579→  if (length(facs) == 0) {
   580→    ons <- if (global) {
   581→      fmrihrf::global_onsets(sframe, onsets(x), fmrihrf::blockids(x))
   582→    } else {
   583→      onsets(x)
   584→    }
   585→    return(list(split(ons, fmrihrf::blockids(x))))
   586→  }
   587→  
   588→  # For categorical events, construct a crossed factor.
   589→  facs <- lapply(facs, function(fac) unlist(elements(fac)))
   590→  
   591→  f <- function(...) {
   592→    interaction(..., drop = TRUE, sep = ":")
   593→  }
   594→  
   595→  cfac <- try(do.call(f, facs))
   596→  # If error, a more informative error message might be warranted.
   597→  
   598→  ret <- if (global) {
   599→    split(fmrihrf::global_onsets(sframe, onsets(x), fmrihrf::blockids(x)), cfac)
   600→  } else {
   601→    split(onsets(x), cfac)
   602→  }
   603→  
   604→  if (blocksplit) {
   605→    bsplit <- split(fmrihrf::blockids(x), cfac)
   606→    ret <- lapply(seq_along(ret), function(i) {
   607→      split(ret[[i]], bsplit[[i]])
   608→    })
   609→  }
   610→  
   611→  names(ret) <- longnames(x)
   612→  ret
   613→}

   615→## ============================================================================
   616→## Section 10: Convolution and Regressor Generation
   617→## ============================================================================
   618→
   619→#' Convolve HRF with Design Matrix.
   620→#'
   621→#' Convolves a HRF with a design matrix (one column per condition) to produce a
   622→#' list of regressors.
   623→#'
   624→#' @param hrf A function representing the HRF.
   625→#' @param dmat Design matrix (with named columns).
   626→#' @param globons Numeric vector of global onsets.
   627→#' @param durations Numeric vector of event durations.
   628→#' @param summate Logical; if TRUE, summate the convolved HRF (default: TRUE).
   629→#'
   630→#' @return A list of regressors (one for each column).
   631→#' @export
   632→convolve_design <- function(hrf, dmat, globons, durations, summate = TRUE) {
   633→  cond.names <- names(dmat)
   634→  
   635→  # Remove rows with NA values.
   636→  if (any(is.na(dmat)) || any(is.na(globons))) {
   637→    keep <- apply(dmat, 1, function(vals) all(!is.na(vals)))
   638→    keep[is.na(globons)] <- FALSE
   639→    dmat <- dmat[keep, ]
   640→    durations <- durations[keep]
   641→    globons <- globons[keep]
   642→  }
   643→  
   644→  reglist <- purrr::map(1:ncol(dmat), function(i) {
   645→    amp <- dmat[, i][[1]]
   646→    nonzero <- which(amp != 0)
   647→    if (length(nonzero) == 0) {
   648→      # Call Reg directly to create an empty regressor object
   649→      fmrihrf::regressor(onsets = numeric(0), hrf = hrf, amplitude = 0)
   650→    } else {
   651→      # Call Reg directly here as well
   652→      fmrihrf::regressor(onsets = globons[nonzero], hrf = hrf, amplitude = amp[nonzero], duration = durations[nonzero], summate = summate)
   653→    }
   654→  })
   655→  
   656→  reglist
   657→}
   658→
   659→#' Extract regressors for an event term
   660→#'
   661→#' Convolve the event-term design matrix with an HRF and return the
   662→#' resulting regressors.
   663→#'
   664→#' @rdname regressors
   665→#' @param hrf HRF function
   666→#' @param sampling_frame sampling_frame object
   667→#' @param summate Logical; sum HRF responses
   668→#' @param drop.empty Logical; drop empty conditions
   669→#' @export
   670→regressors.event_term <- function(x, hrf, sampling_frame, summate = FALSE, drop.empty = TRUE, ...) {
   671→  globons <- fmrihrf::global_onsets(sampling_frame, x$onsets, x$blockids)
   672→  durations <- x$durations
   673→  blockids <- x$blockids
   674→  nimages <- sum(fmrihrf::blocklens(sampling_frame))
   675→  cnames <- conditions(x)
   676→  dmat <- design_matrix(x, drop.empty)
   677→  ncond <- ncol(dmat)
   678→  
   679→  reg <- convolve_design(hrf, dmat, globons, durations, summate = summate)
   680→  names(reg) <- colnames(dmat)
   681→  reg
   682→}
   683→
   684→#' @method convolve event_term
   685→#' @rdname convolve
   686→#' @export
   687→#' @importFrom tibble as_tibble
   688→#' @importFrom dplyr %>% group_by select do ungroup
   689→convolve.event_term <- function(x, hrf, sampling_frame, drop.empty = TRUE, 
   690→                                summate = TRUE, precision = 0.3, ...) {
   691→  # Check for term_tag attribute (should have been added in realise_event_terms)
   692→  term_tag <- attr(x, "term_tag")
   693→  # --- REMOVED FALLBACK LOGIC FOR term_tag ---
   694→  # If term_tag is NULL (e.g., for Ident()-only terms), make_column_names will handle it 
   695→  # by not prepending a term_tag, resulting in direct variable names.
   696→  # if (is.null(term_tag)) {
   697→  #     warning(sprintf("Missing 'term_tag' attribute on event_term '%s'. Using $varname as fallback.", x$varname %||% "Unnamed"), call.=FALSE)
   698→  #     term_tag <- x$varname %||% "UnnamedTerm"
   699→  # }
   700→  
   701→  # --- Basic Setup --- 
   702→  globons <- fmrihrf::global_onsets(sampling_frame, x$onsets, x$blockids)
   703→  durations <- x$durations
   704→  blockids <- x$blockids
   705→  nimages <- sum(fmrihrf::blocklens(sampling_frame))
   706→  
   707→  # --- Get Unconvolved Design Matrix (dmat) --- 
   708→  # design_matrix handles dropping empty/rank-deficient columns based on drop.empty
   709→  dmat <- design_matrix(x, drop.empty = drop.empty)
   710→  
   711→  # --- Get Base Condition Names from the *actual* matrix columns --- 
   712→  # This ensures names match the columns being convolved
   713→  base_cnames <- colnames(dmat)
   714→  
   715→  # Check if dmat became empty after dropping
   716→  if (ncol(dmat) == 0 || nrow(dmat) == 0) {
   717→      warning(sprintf("Design matrix for term '%s' became empty after dropping. Convolution will result in an empty matrix.", term_tag), call.=FALSE)
   718→      # Proceed to generate names for an empty matrix
   719→      base_cnames <- character(0) # Use empty names
   720→  }
   721→  
   722→  # --- Convolution per Block --- 
   723→  block_ids <- unique(blockids)
   724→  # Precompute global sample times once to avoid buggy blockids handling
   725→  global_samples <- fmrihrf::samples(sampling_frame, global = TRUE)
   726→  sample_blockids <- fmrihrf::blockids(sampling_frame)
   727→  cmat_list <- lapply(block_ids, function(bid) {
   728→    idx <- which(blockids == bid)
   729→    # Ensure we subset the correct dmat based on drop.empty consistency
   730→    dblock <- dmat[idx, , drop = FALSE] 
   731→    globons_block <- globons[idx]
   732→    durations_block <- durations[idx]
   733→    
   734→    # Skip block if dblock is empty (e.g., no events for this term in this block)
   735→    if(nrow(dblock) == 0 || ncol(dblock) == 0) return(NULL) 
   736→    
   737→    reg <- convolve_design(hrf, dblock, globons_block, durations_block, summate = summate)
   738→    
   739→    # FIXED METHOD: Evaluate against all global samples but extract only block-specific rows
   740→    # This preserves temporal accuracy while maintaining correct dimensions
   741→    sam_all <- fmrihrf::samples(sampling_frame, global = TRUE)
   742→    
   743→    # Evaluate regressors against ALL samples (preserves temporal accuracy)
   744→    full_block_mat <- do.call(cbind, lapply(reg, function(r) fmrihrf::evaluate(r, sam_all, precision = precision)))
   745→    
   746→    # Extract only the rows for this specific block (maintains correct dimensions)
   747→    block_lengths <- fmrihrf::blocklens(sampling_frame)
   748→    if (as.integer(bid) == 1) {
   749→      block_start <- 1
   750→      block_end <- block_lengths[1]
   751→    } else {
   752→      block_start <- sum(block_lengths[1:(as.integer(bid)-1)]) + 1
   753→      block_end <- sum(block_lengths[1:as.integer(bid)])
   754→    }
   755→    block_mat <- full_block_mat[block_start:block_end, , drop = FALSE]
   756→    block_mat
   757→  })
   758→  
   759→  # Remove NULLs (from blocks with no events/cols) and rbind
   760→  cmat_list <- Filter(Negate(is.null), cmat_list)
   761→  
   762→  # --- Generate Final Column Names --- 
   763→  nb <- fmrihrf::nbasis(hrf)
   764→  # Use the base_cnames derived directly from the dmat that was convolved
   765→  cn <- make_column_names(term_tag, base_cnames, nb)
   766→  
   767→  # Handle case where convolution results in an empty matrix
   768→  if (length(cmat_list) == 0) {
   769→      warning(sprintf("Convolution resulted in an empty matrix for term '%s\'.\n  Returning tibble with correct names but 0 rows.", term_tag), call.=FALSE)
   770→      # Return empty tibble with correct names and 0 rows
   771→      return(tibble::as_tibble(matrix(numeric(0), nrow=0, ncol=length(cn)), 
   772→                               .name_repair="minimal", .names_minimal = cn))
   773→  }
   774→  cmat <- do.call(rbind, cmat_list)
   775→  
   776→  # Handle add_sum flag if present (set by trialwise)
   777→  if (isTRUE(attr(x, "add_sum"))) {
   778→    if (ncol(cmat) > 0) { # Ensure there are columns to average
   779→      mean_col <- matrix(rowMeans(cmat, na.rm = TRUE), ncol = 1)
   780→      mean_col_name <- make.names(paste0(attr(x, "add_sum_label") %||% term_tag, "_mean"))
   781→      colnames(mean_col) <- mean_col_name
   782→      cmat <- cbind(cmat, mean_col)
   783→      # Update column names to include the new mean column
   784→      cn <- c(cn, mean_col_name)
   785→    } else {
   786→      warning(sprintf("Cannot add sum column for term '%s': no base columns generated.", term_tag))
   787→    }
   788→  }
   789→  
   790→  # Assign names, checking for length consistency
   791→  if (length(cn) == ncol(cmat)) {
   792→    colnames(cmat) <- cn
   793→  } else {
   794→      warning(sprintf("Final column name count (%d) mismatch with convolved matrix columns (%d) for term '%s'. Using generic names.", 
   795→                      length(cn), ncol(cmat), term_tag), call. = FALSE)
   796→      colnames(cmat) <- make.names(paste0("col_", seq_len(ncol(cmat))), unique=TRUE)
   797→  }
   798→  
   799→  # --- Optional Debug Validation --- 
   800→  if (getOption("fmrireg.debug", FALSE)) {
   801→     if (exists("is_valid_heading", mode="function")){
   802→        stopifnot(all(is_valid_heading(colnames(cmat))))
   803→     } else {
   804→        warning("fmrireg.debug=TRUE: is_valid_heading helper not found for validation.")
   805→     }
   806→  }
   807→  
   808→  # --- Return Result --- 
   809→  suppressMessages(tibble::as_tibble(cmat, .name_repair = "minimal"))
   810→}
   811→
   812→## ============================================================================
   813→## Section 11: F-Contrast Computation
   814→## ============================================================================
   815→
   816→#' @export
   817→Fcontrasts.event_term <- function(x, max_inter = 4L, ...) {
   818→
   819→  ## --- helpers -------------------------------------------------------------
   820→  .is_cat <- function(ev) !is_continuous(ev)
   821→  .Dmat   <- function(n) {
   822→      if (n < 2) stop("Need at least 2 levels for contrasts.")
   823→      con <- stats::contr.sum(n)
   824→      colnames(con) <- paste0("c", 1:(n - 1))
   825→      con
   826→  }
   827→  .Cvec   <- function(n) matrix(1, nrow = n, ncol = 1)
   828→
   829→  ## --- preparation ---------------------------------------------------------
   830→  evs_cat <- Filter(.is_cat, x$events)
   831→  if (!length(evs_cat)) stop("No categorical variables found in term '", x$varname, "' for Fcontrasts.", call.=FALSE)
   832→
   833→  C <- lapply(evs_cat, function(ev) .Cvec(length(levels(ev))))
   834→  D <- lapply(evs_cat, function(ev) .Dmat(length(levels(ev))))
   835→  names(C) <- names(D) <- names(evs_cat)
   836→
   837→  # --- Get expected row names in Kronecker order ---------------------------
   838→  cat_levels_list <- lapply(evs_cat, levels)
   839→  # --- build Cartesian product of levels (Kronecker order) ------------------ 
   840→  lvl_grid        <- do.call(expand.grid, cat_levels_list)
   841→  cat_cond_names  <- apply(lvl_grid, 1, paste, collapse = ":")
   842→  expected_rows   <- nrow(lvl_grid)
   843→  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------- 
   844→
   845→  ## --- Compute main effects matrices (without rownames yet) ---------------
   846→  main <- Map(function(i) {
   847→      mat_list <- C
   848→      mat_list[[i]] <- D[[i]] 
   849→      Reduce(kronecker, mat_list)
   850→  }, seq_along(D)) |> 
   851→    stats::setNames(names(evs_cat))
   852→
   853→  ## --- Compute interaction effects matrices (without rownames yet) -------
   854→  final_contrasts_list <- if (length(D) > 1 && length(D) <= max_inter) {
   855→      inter <- unlist(lapply(2:length(D), function(k) {
   856→          combn(length(D), k, simplify = FALSE, FUN = function(ix) {
   857→              mat_list <- C
   858→              mat_list[ix] <- D[ix] 
   859→              M <- Reduce(kronecker, mat_list)
   860→              attr(M, "name") <- paste(names(evs_cat)[ix], collapse=":")
   861→              M
   862→          })
   863→      }), recursive = FALSE)
   864→      names(inter) <- vapply(inter, attr, "", "name")
   865→      c(main, inter)
   866→  } else {
   867→      main
   868→  }
   869→  
   870→  ## --- Assign correct categorical rownames to all matrices -------
   871→  final_contrasts_named <- lapply(seq_along(final_contrasts_list), function(i) {
   872→       M <- final_contrasts_list[[i]]
   873→       mat_name <- names(final_contrasts_list)[i]
   874→       if (!is.matrix(M)) { 
   875→            warning(paste("Skipping rownames for invalid matrix in Fcontrasts list element:", mat_name))
   876→            return(M)
   877→       }
   878→       # Compare nrow(M) to expected rows from CATEGORICAL grid/interaction
   879→       if (nrow(M) == expected_rows) {
   880→            # Use dimnames[[1]] <- assignment (should be correct now)
   881→            dimnames(M)[[1]] <- cat_cond_names
   882→       } else {
   883→           # This warning should be less likely now, but keep for safety
   884→           warning(paste("Dimension mismatch for contrast '", mat_name, "': expected ", 
   885→                         expected_rows, " rows (from categorical interaction), but matrix has ", nrow(M), ". Rownames not assigned."))
   886→       }
   887→       M # Return matrix (modified in place)
   888→  })
   889→  
   890→  names(final_contrasts_named) <- names(final_contrasts_list)
   891→  
   892→  final_contrasts_named
   893→}
   894→
   895→#' Retrieve contrast definitions for an event term
   896→#'
   897→#' This accessor returns the list of contrast specifications attached to the
   898→#' term's originating hrfspec, if any.
   899→#'
   900→#' @param x An `event_term` object.
   901→#' @param ... Unused.
   902→#' @return A list of contrast specifications or `NULL` when none are defined.
   903→#' @export
   904→contrasts.event_term <- function(x, ...) {
   905→  hrfspec <- attr(x, "hrfspec")
   906→  if (is.null(hrfspec)) return(NULL)
   907→  hrfspec$contrasts
   908→}
   909→
   910→## ============================================================================
   911→## Section 12: Design Matrix Construction
   912→## ============================================================================
   913→
   914→#' @method design_matrix event_term
   915→#' @rdname design_matrix
   916→#' @export
   917→#' @importFrom tibble as_tibble
   918→design_matrix.event_term <- function(x, drop.empty = TRUE, ...) {
   919→
   920→  # --- Special case: term contains only one continuous event --- 
   921→  # This includes single numeric variables and multi-column basis functions.
   922→  # Bypass model.matrix and return the value matrix directly.
   923→  if (is_continuous(x) && length(x$events) == 1) {
   924→    ev      <- x$events[[1]]
   925→    # Directly use the value matrix (N x K)
   926→    out_mat <- ev$value 
   927→    # Ensure it's a data frame before naming
   928→    out_df <- as.data.frame(out_mat) # Convert matrix to data frame
   929→    
   930→    # Use columns() to get the base condition tags for naming intermediate matrix
   931→    # These tags represent the *parts* of the final name (e.g., "01", "02" for Poly)
   932→    # Do NOT sanitize with make.names here, as these are intermediate component names.
   933→    intermediate_cond_tags <- try(columns(ev), silent = TRUE)
   934→
   935→    if (inherits(intermediate_cond_tags, "try-error") || length(intermediate_cond_tags) != ncol(out_df)) {
   936→      warning(sprintf("Failed to get valid condition tags via columns() or column count mismatch for event term '%s' (varname: '%s'). Using generic V# names for intermediate matrix.", 
   937→                      x$varname %||% "UnnamedTerm", ev$varname %||% "UnnamedEventVar"), call. = FALSE)
   938→      # Fallback to generic, sanitized names if columns() fails or gives wrong number
   939→      cnames <- make.names(paste0("V", seq_len(ncol(out_df))), unique = TRUE) 
   940→    } else {
   941→      # Use the raw condition tags (e.g., "01", "02") directly as intermediate names.
   942→      # These are not necessarily valid full R names yet but are components.
   943→      cnames <- intermediate_cond_tags 
   944→    }
   945→    
   946→    names(out_df) <- cnames
   947→    # Return as a tibble
   948→    return(tibble::as_tibble(out_df, .name_repair = "minimal"))
   949→  }
   950→  
   951→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
   952→  ## 1. Build the "data" data-frame that model.matrix() needs
   953→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
   954→  #   * For categorical events -> factor column (1 per event)
   955→  #   * For continuous events  -> numeric column(s) (one per matrix column)
   956→  
   957→  # Helper to extract appropriate column(s) from an event object
   958→  build_cols <- function(ev) {
   959→    if (is_categorical(ev)) {
   960→      # Categorical: return data frame with factor column named ev$varname
   961→      fac <- factor(ev$value[, 1L],
   962→                    levels = seq_along(ev$meta$levels),
   963→                    labels = ev$meta$levels)
   964→      df_out <- data.frame(fac, check.names=FALSE)
   965→      colnames(df_out) <- ev$varname 
   966→      df_out
   967→    } else {
   968→      # Continuous/Basis: return data frame with a single matrix column named ev$varname
   969→      mat_col <- ev$value # This is the N x K matrix
   970→      df_out <- data.frame(I(mat_col)) # Use I() to store matrix in one column
   971→      colnames(df_out) <- ev$varname # Name the column containing the matrix
   972→      df_out
   973→    }
   974→  }
   975→  
   976→  # Combine columns from all events into a single data frame
   977→  # cbind should now handle the mix of factor columns and matrix columns
   978→  df_list <- lapply(x$events, build_cols)
   979→  df <- try(do.call(cbind, df_list), silent=TRUE)
   980→  if (inherits(df, "try-error")) {
   981→      stop("Failed to construct data frame for model.matrix from event term: ", x$varname, 
   982→           "\n  Original error: ", attr(df, "condition")$message)
   983→  }
   984→  
   985→  # === DEBUG PRINT ===
   986→  #message(sprintf("Term: %s, nrow(df): %d, length(x$onsets): %d", x$varname, nrow(df), length(x$onsets)))
   987→  # === END DEBUG ===
   988→  
   989→  if (nrow(df) != length(x$onsets)) {
   990→      stop("Internal error: Row mismatch when building data frame for event term: ", x$varname)
   991→  }
   992→
   993→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
   994→  ## 2. model.matrix() with the term's formula
   995→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
   996→  # Get the formula (e.g., ~ Condition:Modulator - 1)
   997→  form <- formula(x)
   998→  
   999→  # Special case: Check for single-level factors which cause model.matrix to fail
  1000→  # If any factor column has only one level, handle it specially
  1001→  has_single_level_factor <- FALSE
  1002→  for (col_name in colnames(df)) {
  1003→    col_data <- df[[col_name]]
  1004→    if (is.factor(col_data) && nlevels(col_data) <= 1) {
  1005→      has_single_level_factor <- TRUE
  1006→      break
  1007→    }
  1008→  }
  1009→  
  1010→  if (has_single_level_factor) {
  1011→    # For single-level factors, create a simple matrix of ones
  1012→    # This represents the constant effect of that single level
  1013→    n_rows <- nrow(df)
  1014→    mm <- matrix(1, nrow = n_rows, ncol = 1)
  1015→    # Use the condition name from conditions() for naming
  1016→    expected_colnames_raw <- conditions(x, drop.empty = FALSE)
  1017→    if (length(expected_colnames_raw) > 0) {
  1018→      colnames(mm) <- make.names(expected_colnames_raw[1], unique = TRUE)
  1019→    } else {
  1020→      colnames(mm) <- make.names(x$varname, unique = TRUE)
  1021→    }
  1022→  } else {
  1023→    # Normal case: use model.matrix
  1024→    # Ensure the data frame column names match what the formula expects
  1025→    # (formula uses original names, df uses sanitized/numbered names)
  1026→    # model.matrix should handle this via the data=df argument.
  1027→    
  1028→    mm <- try(model.matrix(form, data = df), silent=TRUE)
  1029→    if (inherits(mm, "try-error")) {
  1030→         stop("Failed to create model matrix for event term: ", x$varname, 
  1031→              "\n  Formula was: ", deparse(form),
  1032→              "\n  Data frame columns: ", paste(colnames(df), collapse=", "),
  1033→              "\n  Original error: ", attr(mm, "condition")$message)
  1034→    }
  1035→    
  1036→    # --- SET COLNAMES using conditions() as the single source of truth --- 
  1037→    # Get potentially *all* condition names first (before drop.empty)
  1038→    expected_colnames_raw <- conditions(x, drop.empty = FALSE)
  1039→    
  1040→    # Sanitize the raw names using make.names
  1041→    expected_colnames <- make.names(expected_colnames_raw, unique = TRUE)
  1042→    
  1043→    # Basic check: Does the number of columns match?
  1044→    # model.matrix might produce fewer columns if rank-deficient, 
  1045→    # but conditions() should produce the full set based on expand.grid.
  1046→    # This mismatch needs careful handling. For now, assume model.matrix is correct
  1047→    # in terms of *which* columns are estimable, and use conditions() to name them.
  1048→    # If ncol(mm) < length(expected_colnames), it implies model.matrix dropped some.
  1049→    
  1050→    # TODO: How to robustly map expected_colnames to the columns present in mm?
  1051→    # This is tricky. model.matrix column names (e.g., ConditionB:Modulator1)
  1052→    # don't directly map to conditions() output (e.g., Condition[B]:Modulator[1]).
  1053→    # For now, we ASSUME the order is the same and the number of columns matches 
  1054→    # *if the design is full rank*. If not, this naming will be wrong.
  1055→    # A more robust solution might involve parsing model.matrix colnames or attributes.
  1056→    # Let's proceed with the direct assignment as per the reviewer's suggestion, 
  1057→    # but acknowledge this fragility.
  1058→    if (ncol(mm) != length(expected_colnames)) {
  1059→        warning(sprintf("Column count mismatch for '%s': model.matrix (%d) vs conditions (%d). Naming may be incorrect due to rank deficiency.",
  1060→                        x$varname, ncol(mm), length(expected_colnames_raw)), call. = FALSE)
  1061→        # Attempt to name the existing columns anyway, hoping the order matches
  1062→        # This might fail if length(expected_colnames) is shorter, though unlikely.
  1063→        colnames(mm) <- expected_colnames[1:ncol(mm)] 
  1064→    } else {
  1065→        colnames(mm) <- expected_colnames
  1066→    }
  1067→  }
  1068→  
  1069→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
  1070→  ## 3. Drop empty columns if requested (optional)
  1071→  ## ----------------------------------------------------------------
  1072→  # model.matrix might return fewer columns than expected if interactions 
  1073→  # lead to rank deficiency. drop.empty applies to the *output* matrix.
  1074→  if (isTRUE(drop.empty)) {
  1075→      # Check for intercept columns and constant columns
  1076→      # Intercept columns are named "(Intercept)" 
  1077→      # Constant columns have zero variance but non-zero values (e.g., all ones)
  1078→      is_intercept <- (colnames(mm) == "(Intercept)")
  1079→      
  1080→      # Calculate variance and check for all-zero columns
  1081→      col_vars <- apply(mm, 2, var, na.rm = TRUE)
  1082→      col_all_zero <- colSums(abs(mm), na.rm = TRUE) == 0
  1083→      
  1084→      # A column should be kept if:
  1085→      # 1. It's an intercept column, OR
  1086→      # 2. It has non-zero variance (not constant), OR  
  1087→      # 3. It's a constant non-zero column (zero variance but not all zeros)
  1088→      is_constant_nonzero <- (col_vars < 1e-8 | is.na(col_vars)) & !col_all_zero
  1089→      keep_cols <- which(is_intercept | col_vars > 1e-8 | is.na(col_vars) | is_constant_nonzero)
  1090→      
  1091→      if (length(keep_cols) < ncol(mm)){
  1092→          # message("Dropping empty columns: ", paste(colnames(mm)[! (1:ncol(mm)) %in% keep_cols], collapse=", "))
  1093→          mm <- mm[, sort(keep_cols), drop = FALSE]
  1094→      }
  1095→  }
  1096→  
  1097→  # Optional: Handle NAs by zero-filling (historical behavior)
  1098→  # mm[is.na(mm)] <- 0 
  1099→
  1100→  tibble::as_tibble(mm, .name_repair = "check_unique")
  1101→}

  1107→#' Print fmri_term objects.
  1108→#'
  1109→#' @param x An fmri_term object.
  1110→#' @param ... Additional arguments.
  1111→#' @export
  1112→#' @rdname print
  1113→print.fmri_term <- function(x, ...) {
  1114→  cat("fmri_term: ", class(x)[[1]], "\n")
  1115→  cat("  Term Name: ", x$varname, "\n")
  1116→  cat("  Num Rows: ", nrow(design_matrix(x)), "\n")
  1117→  cat("  Num Columns: ", ncol(design_matrix(x)), "\n")
  1118→}
  1119→
  1120→#' Print convolved_term objects.
  1121→#'
  1122→#' @param x A convolved_term object.
  1123→#' @param ... Additional arguments.
  1124→#' @export
  1125→#' @rdname print
  1126→print.convolved_term <- function(x, ...) {
  1127→  cat("fmri_term: ", class(x)[[1]], "\n")
  1128→  cat("  Term Name: ", x$varname, "\n")
  1129→  cat("  Formula:  ", as.character(formula(x$evterm)), "\n")
  1130→  cat("  Num Events: ", nrow(x$evterm$event_table), "\n")
  1131→  cat("  Num Rows: ", nrow(design_matrix(x)), "\n")
  1132→  cat("  Num Columns: ", ncol(design_matrix(x)), "\n")
  1133→  cat("  Conditions: ", conditions(x), "\n")
  1134→  cat("  Term Types: ", paste(purrr::map_chr(x$evterm$events, ~ class(.)[[1]])), "\n")
  1135→}

  1137→#' Print afni_hrf_convolved_term objects.
